headline: Sarawak Peat Fires Destroy 45,000 Hectares in 3 Months
context: Prolonged dry conditions led to peat fires destroying 45,000 hectares across 8 Sarawak divisions between January and March 2026.

cards.big: Peat fires destroyed 45,000 hectares across 8 Sarawak divisions in Q1 2026
cards.sub: Air quality exceeded hazardous levels in Sibu and Mukah for 28 consecutive days

cards.big: Damage estimated at RM1.8 billion including crop losses and healthcare costs
cards.sub: 58,000 residents evacuated from 12 affected communities in Mukah and Dalat

cards.big: 83% of fire hotspots originated on drained peatland converted for agriculture
cards.sub: Drainage canals lowered water tables by 1.2m making peat highly combustible

cards.big: BOMBA deployed 2,400 firefighters but equipment shortage limited response
cards.sub: Sarawak has only 3 water-bombing aircraft versus recommended minimum of 8

cards.big: Peat fires test whether agricultural drainage policies account for fire risk
45,000 hectares of peat burned in 3 months. Sarawak's fire crisis needs prevention, not response.
